action.hypothesis: After bucketed, plain two-stage, routed, and gated soft-codebook backbones all failed to beat the current `K=32, EF=16` bucketed point, the next severe-stagnation move should test a genuinely different existing architecture rather than return to scalar tweaks. `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` is the strongest remaining soft-codebook-adjacent candidate by prior quality and may transfer better to `EF=16` at `K=32` by allocating capacity asymmetrically across stages instead of via buckets, routing, or gating.
action.summary: Run an architecture-only comparison at `K=32, EF=16` with `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. This follows the severe-stagnation guidance to stop minor tweaks and test a fundamentally different existing architecture on the most valuable exposed small-K frontier slot.
action.self_review: This is a policy-clean architecture-only move on the same high-value `K=32` target under fixed `EF=16`. I chose the asymmetric two-stage variant because it offers a real structural departure from the already-failed bucketed, routed, plain, and gated branches while still having a stronger prior than older discarded non-soft-codebook families.
notes_to_memory: Round 140 continues severe-stagnation recovery by testing `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=32, EF=16` after the bucketed, plain two-stage, routed, and gated soft-codebook variants all failed to improve the current frontier point.
Keep optimizer, LR, and aux fixed so the result isolates architecture alone on the highest-value exposed small-K slot.
This run asks whether asymmetric stage allocation is a better inductive bias than bucketing, routing, or gating under the current EF=16 regime.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` improves the `K=32, EF=16` point, propagate it to `K=64` before returning to any recipe tuning.
If it is close but slightly worse at `K=32`, consider one preprocessing or optimizer probe on this asymmetric backbone rather than discarding it immediately.

If it clearly loses at `K=32`, treat the remaining soft-codebook architecture space as close to exhausted under the current recipe and pivot to under-explored runtime axes on the existing bucketed backbone.

This follows the severe-stagnation guidance to stop minor tweaks and test a fundamentally different existing architecture on the most valuable exposed small-K frontier slot.
result.self_review: This is a policy-clean architecture-only move on the same high-value `K=32` target under fixed `EF=16`. I chose the asymmetric two-stage variant because it offers a real structural departure from the already-failed bucketed, routed, plain, and gated branches while still having a stronger prior than older discarded non-soft-codebook families.
